message:
feat(config): implement real schemars-backed JSON schema output

Replaces the stub pt config schema command with real JSON Schema
generation using the schemars crate, enabling tooling and editor
integration to validate policy and priors configuration files.

Changes:

1. pt-config crate:
   - Add schemars 1.2.0 dependency with chrono04 feature
   - Derive JsonSchema on all 25 policy types (Policy, Guardrails,
     PatternEntry, LossMatrix, RobotMode, FdrControl, etc.)
   - Derive JsonSchema on all 20 priors types (Priors, ClassPriors,
     ClassParams, BetaParams, GammaParams, DirichletParams, etc.)

2. pt-core/src/main.rs:
   - New generate_config_schema() function using schemars::schema_for!
     to produce JSON Schema for "priors" and "policy" configs
   - New run_config_schema() replacing the stub with real output,
     supporting JSON, summary, and pretty-print output formats
   - Update help text: "priors, policy, capabilities" → "priors or
     policy" (capabilities was never a config file)
   - Three new tests: policy schema exposes guardrails, priors schema
     exposes classes, unknown name rejected

3. test/pt_config.bats:
   - Policy schema test updated from stub assertions to real JSON
     Schema validation (type=object, title=Policy, guardrails $ref,
     protected_patterns array)
   - New test: priors schema validates type, title, classes $ref

Closes beads issue bd-tn92.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-te4m","title":"Port Help overlay to ftui Modal + Paragraph","description":"# Port Help Overlay to ftui Modal

## What
Migrate the Help overlay (currently rendered inline in app.rs) to use ftui Modal +
Paragraph widgets. The help screen shows keyboard shortcuts and is toggled with "?".

## Current Help Implementation
The help overlay is rendered as a full-screen overlay with a bordered block containing
formatted text. Two versions exist:
- **Full help**: For terminals ≥120 cols, shows all keybindings in categorized sections
- **Compact help**: For smaller terminals, abbreviated keybinding list

### Help Content Categories
- Navigation: j/k, Up/Down, Home/End, PgUp/PgDn, Ctrl+u/d
- Selection: Space, a, A, u, x
- Search: /, Enter, Esc
- Views: Enter (detail), s/t/g (sub-views), v (goal)
- Actions: e (execute), r (refresh)
- General: ? (help), q (quit)

## ftui Implementation
- Use `Modal` for the overlay container (centered, dimmed backdrop)
- Use `Paragraph` with styled `Line`/`Span` for the help text
- Use `Scrollbar` if help text exceeds modal height
- Adaptive: check `frame.area().width` to choose full vs compact layout

## Enhancement Opportunity
ftui's CommandPalette widget could complement or replace the help overlay — it provides
fuzzy-searchable command listing with Bayesian scoring. Consider adding as future
enhancement (not in this bead's scope).

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — help rendering moved to dedicated function/widget
- Possibly new `tui/widgets/help_overlay.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Help overlay renders as centered modal with "?" toggle
- All keybindings displayed in categorized sections
- Compact layout for small terminals
- Esc or "?" dismisses the overlay
- Styled text with keybinding highlights","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:11:41.307922021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:54:39.531999561Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:54:39.531920975Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui","widget"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-te4m","depends_on_id":"bd-2hmx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-te4m","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-te4m","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-tlng","title":"[bd-ppcl.3] Implement cwd/repo/worktree resolver with negative-path coverage","description":"Implement the actual resolver that maps a process cwd and surrounding filesystem context to repo/workspace evidence, including non-repo directories, detached worktrees, nested repos, and stale/deleted directories.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:41:47.400014946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T20:45:10.415855891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T20:45:10.415200588Z","close_reason":"Complete: workspace_resolver module in pt-core/collect with find_repo_root, read_head_state, resolve_workspace_for_pid, worktree detection, negative-path handling (deleted cwd, permission denied, non-repo), 10 tests all passing including live PID test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["collect","git","provenance","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tlng","depends_on_id":"bd-1oi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:42:23.839763312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-tlng","author":"ubuntu","text":"Purpose: turn the normalized evidence model into a real collector with trustworthy negative-path behavior. This subtask should include fixture-heavy tests for active repo, nested repo, detached worktree, deleted cwd, permission failure, and non-repo cases, plus structured logs that show why resolution succeeded, degraded, or failed.","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:41:47Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-trka","title":"Sanitize NaN user-intent weights","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:56:16.690113189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:00:09.269057540Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T03:00:09.266640494Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-txk2","title":"[bd-ppcl.2] Add provenance migration, corruption, and omission-path regression coverage","description":"Add the persistence-side regression suite for schema migration, corrupted provenance payloads, partial exports, and omission/redaction cases so replay remains trustworthy under real failure modes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:04:25.718152860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T13:05:45.131272894Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T13:05:45.130600352Z","close_reason":"Complete: provenance_persistence_regression.rs with 12 tests covering JSON round-trip fidelity, corrupted/truncated/empty JSON detection, missing required fields, redacted evidence preservation, empty graph validity, schema version mismatch detection, forward-compatible extra fields, summary count consistency, bundle serialization with integrity hash stability, and provenance ID stability across serialization.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["migration","provenance","subtask","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-txk2","depends_on_id":"bd-hn19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:05:27.994244077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-txk2","depends_on_id":"bd-ppcl.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:05:29.945110879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-txk2","depends_on_id":"bd-zflp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:05:26.079572100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-txk2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Why this exists:\nPersistence work fails in the field through migration mistakes, partial writes, corruption, and intentionally omitted sections, not just through happy-path serialization bugs. This bead captures those failure modes as first-class regression targets.\n\nScope:\n- Add corruption, truncation, version-skew, omission, and redaction-path coverage.\n- Require detailed debug logs that pinpoint whether failure came from schema drift, storage damage, or policy-driven omission.\n- Treat replay fidelity as a contract, not a best-effort aspiration.\n\nAcceptance intent:\nFuture changes to provenance persistence should break tests immediately if they jeopardize replay, migration safety, or operator diagnosability.","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:08:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-u8wy","title":"Align wrapper version test with current pt version","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:51:03.328017420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:53:12.524522500Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T17:53:12.523642567Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -252,10 +252,28 @@ teardown() {
     local schema
     schema=$(pt config schema --file policy 2>/dev/null)
 
-    # Schema printing is currently a stub; enforce that it fails closed but returns structured JSON.
-    run jq -e '.schema_version, .session_id, .generated_at, .status, .message' <<<"$schema"
+    run jq -e '.type == "object"' <<<"$schema"
+    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
+    run jq -e '.title == "Policy"' <<<"$schema"
+    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
+    run jq -e '(.properties.guardrails["$ref"] == "#/$defs/Guardrails") or (.properties.guardrails.type == "object")' <<<"$schema"
+    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
+    run jq -e '.["$defs"].Guardrails.properties.protected_patterns.type == "array"' <<<"$schema"
+    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
+}
+
+@test "pt config schema --file priors is valid JSON schema" {
+    skip_if_no_jq
+
+    local schema
+    schema=$(pt config schema --file priors 2>/dev/null)
+
+    run jq -e '.type == "object"' <<<"$schema"
+    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
+    run jq -e '.title == "Priors"' <<<"$schema"
+    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
+    run jq -e '(.properties.classes["$ref"] == "#/$defs/ClassPriors") or (.properties.classes.type == "object")' <<<"$schema"
     [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
-    [[ "$output" == *"Schema for policy"* ]]
 }
 
 @test "pt config show --file policy includes protected_patterns" {
